Breezy Boomers.Profile: Breezy Boomers fans are confident and easygoing consumers who enjoy the rewards of a stable financial situation. They gravitate toward brands that offer convenience, reliability, and a sense of familiarity, favouring products or services that enhance their leisure-focused lifestyle. Tech-capable but not overly dependent on digital trends, they appreciate straightforward communication and value-for-money offerings that cater to their preferences for comfort and quality.
Breezy Boomers.Consumer Attributes:
”Breezy Boomer" fans:
Relaxed and Confident: Approaches life with a laid-back attitude, valuing comfort and ease in their choices.
Financially Stable: Likely to have a steady income or retirement savings, allowing for discretionary spending on leisure and quality.
Experience-Oriented: Prefers enjoyable experiences over material possessions, such as travel, dining, or social activities.
Value-Conscious: Looks for good value without compromising on quality, favouring trusted and reliable brands.
Cautiously Tech-Capable: Comfortable using technology for convenience (e.g., online shopping or smart devices) but approaches new tech trends with caution, preferring familiar tools and platforms.
Health and Wellness Aware: Interested in products and services that promote well-being, comfort, and an active lifestyle.
Community-Focused: Enjoys being part of social groups or engaging in community events, whether locally or through online communities.
Appreciates Simplicity: Favours straightforward, no-frills communication and solutions that align with their relaxed, low-stress outlook.
Breezy Boomers.Bio: Robert and Susan are Fremantle to the core and love being part of the Purple Army, with Robert now a 20+ year member.  They live a comfortable and indulgent lifestyle having downsized to a low maintenance townhouse close to cafes, restaurants, museums, galleries and the theatre. With ample time and financial resources from superannuation and a healthy share portfolio, they enjoy going to the football while also appreciating the finer things in life.  Now that their kids are young adults and have left the nest, they have the freedom to explore their passions and interests which include good food and wine and embracing the arts and they are regular visitors to Perth’s Cultural Centre.  Robert comes from a large Italian family and enjoys nothing more than celebrating family events over big Italian lunches at restaurants like La Sosta (one of his favourite places) and talking up Freo’s chances of winning a flag.
Breezy Boomers.About Me:
Being able to support Fremantle as member is important to me.
When it comes to new technology, I am not an early adopter.
I’m proud of my heritage and culture.
When I buy, I’m looking for good quality at a fair price.
Being able to enjoy the finer things in life is important to me.

Breezy Boomers.Cultural Diversity: Moderate: The Breezy Boomer have a moderate level of cultural diversity with a strong representation of second-generation Australians with a European heritage particularly Italy and Greece.  The Breezy Boomers have a higher percentage of males to the Australian population but slightly less than the Fremantle member base and index high on females.  Religious affiliations are less diverse with a bias towards Christianity.
Breezy Boomers.Sustainability: Moderate: Breezy Boomers fans are aware of the importance of Sustainability and are reasonably proactive in supporting Sustainability initiatives. They invest in solar panels, conserve water, recycle diligently, and take particular care around household chemicals – reading labels carefully and disposing of hazardous products responsibly. Breezy Boomers can often be seen volunteering for community clean-up events near where they live.
Breezy Boomers.Political Values: Conservative: Breezy Boomers fans hold strong and settled political views. Firmly conservative in outlook, they back parties that reflect their core beliefs – individual freedom, private enterprise, and a robust national defence are non-negotiable priorities for this segment.
Breezy Boomers.Outlook on Life: Slightly Negative to Neutral: The Breezy Boomers have a neutral Outlook on Life, trending towards slightly negative.  They see themselves as generally a bit better off than most but are not overly positive about the future even though they are generally good in health. Breezy Boomers expect little to change from the way things are for themselves and the world.

Comfortable Crowd.Profile: Comfortable Crowd fans are a high-income professional enjoying the fruits of a stable and successful career. They prioritise quality in their purchases, preferring premium brands and experiences that complement their affluent, comfortable lifestyle. This group tends to value convenience, luxury, and exclusivity in the products and services they choose, often spending on travel, fine dining, and home upgrades.
Comfortable Crowd.Consumer Attributes:
”Comfortable Crowd" fans:
Affluent and Secure: Enjoys a high level of financial stability, often associated with successful careers and a strong socio-economic position.
Quality-Oriented: Prefers premium products and services, prioritising quality, durability, and exclusivity in their purchases.
Convenience-Driven: Values products and services that save time or enhance efficiency, reflecting a busy yet refined lifestyle.
Experience-Focused: Seeks out indulgent and memorable experiences such as luxury travel, fine dining, or cultural events.
Home and Lifestyle Investors: Likely to spend on home improvements, décor, and modern conveniences to elevate their living environment.
Brand Loyal: Shows a preference for established and reputable brands, emphasising trust and reliability in their choices.
Selective Tech Adoption: Adopts technology that integrates seamlessly into their lives, focusing on practical and functional advancements over trendy innovations.
Discerning and Thoughtful: Makes deliberate purchasing decisions, often conducting research or seeking recommendations to ensure value and alignment with their sophisticated tastes.
Comfortable Crowd.Bio: Andy is a died in the wool Freo fan and has been part of the Purple Army as a member for 18 years.   Andy is a self-made man having worked his way up from on the tools in the industrial company he works for and is now a senior executive in the same company.  He and his wife Sharon rarely miss a game and when they are travelling on holidays, they stream the games live.  Andy does what he can to help the environment and justified keeping his Porsche Cayenne by buying Sharon a Tesla.  Andy keeps fit by walking every day and joining the army of MAMILs on weekends for a 3-4 hour bike ride.  Andy lives a comfortable life in his and Sharon’s dream home and they fund their lifestyle from their investments in shares and property as well as his well-paid executive income.  Andy is a passionate member and lives for the day he will see his mighty Dockers raise the premiership cup!
Comfortable Crowd.About Me:
I love being part of the Purple Army
I believe that success comes to those who earn it.
I’m always looking for new things to experience
When I buy, I’m looking for quality at a fair price
Being able to enjoy the finer things in life is important to me

Comfortable Crowd.Cultural Diversity: Moderate to High: The Comfortable Crowd have a fair level of cultural diversity, with an above average proportion born overseas or  second-generation Australians with European heritage.  The Comfortable Crowd have a higher percentage of males to the Australian population and slightly more than the Fremantle member base.  Religious affiliations are less diverse with a bias towards Christianity.
Comfortable Crowd.Sustainability: High: Comfortable Crowd fans are aware of the importance of Sustainability and are proactive in supporting initiatives. Willing to volunteer and absorb personal cost to make a difference, they invest in solar, drive hybrid or electric vehicles, buy organic, and will readily pay a premium for genuinely carbon neutral products. You will also find them directing funds toward ethical investments, practising yoga or meditation, and lending their voice to environmental and social movements.
Comfortable Crowd.Political Values: Conservative: Comfortable Crowd fans hold clear political views and are rarely shy about sharing them. Conservative by inclination, they favour parties that champion individual freedom, reward enterprise, and take a firm stance on national security and defence.
Comfortable Crowd.Outlook on Life: Positive: The Comfortable Crowd have a positive Outlook on Life. They enjoy excellent health and see themselves as better off than most. The Comfortable Crowd are are positive about the future, seeing Australia heading in the right direction and optimistic about the world in general.

Founding Faithfuls.Profile: Founding Faithful fans are loyal, community-oriented individuals who value tradition and simplicity. They have a stable lifestyle, often rooted in long-standing habits and preferences, with spending focused on home maintenance, health needs, and occasional leisure activities. Strongly tied to their past experiences, they appreciate brands that evoke trust, reliability, and a sense of belonging.
Founding Faithfuls.Consumer Attributes:
”Founding Faithful" fans:
Loyal and Traditional: Strongly values long-term relationships with brands, services, and organisations, preferring familiar and reliable options.
Community-Oriented: Engages in local activities and supports causes that promote neighbourhood or community well-being.
Financially Conservative: Prioritises practical and essential spending, focusing on home maintenance, health services, and other necessities.
Emotionally Invested: Often nostalgic, they resonate with brands or products that align with their personal history or cultural identity.
Home-Focused Lifestyle: Enjoys hobbies and activities centered around the home, such as gardening, DIY projects, and family gatherings.
Cautious Adopter: Prefers tried-and-true methods over new or unproven innovations, often relying on recommendations from trusted sources.
Value Simplicity: Appreciates straightforward communication and functional products without unnecessary complexities.
Resilient and Grounded: Maintains a pragmatic outlook on life, favouring stability and long-term benefits over short-term trends.
Founding Faithfuls.Bio: Geoff is relishing retirement with his partner Linda, after 40+ years working in engineering.  He and Linda have worked hard to secure their future settling into a nice house in the outer suburbs with a big garden and a work shed. Now they are retired, they are final taking that big caravanning holiday they have been planning for years. Geoff lived and worked in the Fremantle area most of his life and played local footy until life, kids and work (and a dodgy knee) forced him to hang up the boots.  While he went to the local footy, he loved watching the Dockers play and before he knew it, he was part of the Purple Army.  Geoff gets along to a as many games as he can with Linda when she’s up for it, his mates, or his son Daniel.  He loves the experience of singing the Club song with the faithful in the outer after a big win, which is even better when he’s had a punt on game.  Geoff remains quite active with DIY projects, tending their garden and woodworking in his shed while listening to the mighty Dockers on the radio.
Founding Faithfuls.About Me:
I enjoy being with people, but I enjoy working on my own
I like to be able to do things myself rather than pay someone else
I’m enjoying life now that I am retired.
I’m a proud Aussie that is loyal to my family and friends
Although I’m not rich, I like a bit of punt and celebrating a win.

Founding Faithfuls.Cultural Diversity: Moderate: The Founding Faithfuls have a moderate level of cultural diversity with a strong representation of second-generation Australians with a European heritage particularly Italy, Greece and Germany.  The Founding Faithfuls have a higher percentage of males to the Australian population but slightly less than the Fremantle member base and index higher on females.  Religious affiliations are less diverse with a bias towards Christianity.
Founding Faithfuls.Sustainability: Moderate to High: Founding Faithfuls fans are aware of the importance of Sustainability and do what they can to minimise their environmental impact. Their approach is practical and domestic – generating solar electricity, buying from renewable energy providers, carefully reading chemical labels, safely disposing of household hazardous waste, and diligently reducing the rubbish they throw away. On social equity and economic development they are less interested or active.
Founding Faithfuls.Political Values: Moderative to Conservative: Founding Faithfuls fans take a measured interest in political matters. Moderate to conservative in outlook, they are drawn to parties that prioritise stability, uphold traditional Australian values, and protect the way of life they have built and believe in.
Founding Faithfuls.Outlook on Life: Neutral to Negative: The Founding Faithfuls have a neutral to negative Outlook on Life with an expectation that things will probably be worse off for them in the future.  They see themselves as generally worse off than others financially, socially and in their health as they age.  Founding Faithfuls are reasonably pessimistic expecting little will change and if it does it will probably be for the worse.

Generation Fun.Profile: Gen Fun fans are vibrant and adventurous individuals focused on enjoying life and exploring new experiences. They prioritise socialising, entertainment, and travel, often spending on dining out, events, and trendy products that align with their dynamic lifestyle. With a playful approach to life, they embrace brands that are fun, accessible, and resonate with their need for excitement and connection.
Generation Fun.Consumer Attributes:
”Gen Fun" fans:
Youthful and Energetic: Exudes a vibrant, carefree attitude, focusing on enjoying life to the fullest with a playful and adventurous spirit.
Socially Active: Prioritises socialising with friends and participating in group activities, often spending on events, nightlife, and shared experiences.
Experience-Driven: Values spending on entertainment, travel, and activities that provide excitement and memorable moments over material possessions.
Trend-Conscious: Keeps up with the latest trends, favoring brands and products that are stylish, innovative, and resonate with their peers.
Digitally Engaged: Heavily active on social media platforms, using them for entertainment, connecting with others, and discovering new products.
Price-Sensitive but Impulsive: Budget-conscious but willing to splurge on experiences or items that offer instant gratification and enjoyment.
Tech-Savvy: Early adopters of apps, gadgets, and services that simplify their lives or enhance their ability to connect and share experiences.
Brand Fluid: Open to experimenting with new and emerging brands, valuing creativity and authenticity over long-term loyalty.
Generation Fun.Bio: Tom became a Freo fan while in primary school after going to a game with his parents when he watched Matthew Pavlich tear up Subiaco kicking six goals and leading the Dockers to a big win over the Kangaroos.  The euphoric experience of being part of the Purple Army ensured there would be no other club for Tom.  Tom recently graduated with a commerce degree after deferring for a couple of years to back-pack his way around Europe. Since getting back he’s moved in with his folks.  The house is big enough that he doesn’t feel the need to move out, and life is pretty good when mum and dad are covering the bills (especially when you have a HECS debt as big as Tom’s).  Tom is in his mid 20’s and thinks he’ll live at home until he has settled into a full- time job.  Life is big and hectic for Tom who is always on the go looking for the next big experience.  When he’s not at the footy, out partying, or working as a barman, he’s off surfing with his mates.  But if the Dockers make the finals – he’ll be there!
Generation Fun.About Me:
I consider myself a passionate Freo fan.
I enjoy being the centre of attention.
I want my life to be full of excitement.
I refuse to spend my money unless I have to.
I do a lot to maintain excellent physical health.

Generation Fun.Cultural Diversity: Moderate to High: Gen Fun members have a moderate to high level of cultural diversity with good representation of people born outside Australia.  Gen Fun have a higher percentage of males to the Australian population and slightly more than the Fremantle member base.  Religious affiliations are also diverse which is consistent with the diverse cultural background of Gen Fun members.
Generation Fun.Sustainability: High: Gen Fun fans have a high level of awareness and interest in Sustainability and social justice. Socially conscious and environmentally driven, they are most likely to join movements, campaign for social causes, and back politicians with genuine green credentials. Minimising their environmental impact matters – but so does spending with purpose, choosing ethical brands, paying a premium for carbon neutral products, and directing investments toward sustainable funds.
Generation Fun.Political Values: Progressive: Gen Fun fans are confidently progressive in their politics, with strong values and little hesitation in backing parties – or independents – who deliver on green policy and genuine social good over those simply flying the flag of the major parties.
Generation Fun.Outlook on Life: Positive: Gen Fun members have a pretty positive Outlook on Life and see themselves as generally pretty lucky to enjoy the life they lead. Gen Fun members are optimistic about the future both for themselves and the world seeing the world as their oyster.  They think they are and will continue to be better off than others.  They are generally very good in health and lead an active and healthy lifestyle.

Happy Suburbanites.Profile: Happy Suburbanites fans enjoy a comfortable and balanced lifestyle, focusing on family, community, and everyday pleasures. They value practicality and affordability, often spending on home improvement, family outings, and hobbies like gardening or DIY projects. With a strong connection to their suburban roots, they prefer brands that offer reliability, convenience, and a sense of familiarity.
Happy Suburbanites.Consumer Attributes:
”Happy Suburbanite" fans:
Family-Oriented: Places a strong emphasis on family life, prioritising spending that supports family activities and home needs.
Practical and Budget-Conscious: Seeks affordable, reliable products and services that offer good value without compromising quality.
Suburban Roots: Enjoys a stable, community-driven lifestyle in suburban areas, often valuing local businesses and services.
Home Improvement Enthusiast: Likely to invest in DIY projects, gardening, and home upgrades that enhance their living environment.
Socially Engaged: Participates in neighbourhood or community events, fostering a strong sense of connection with those around them.
Comfort-Focused: Prioritises products and experiences that offer convenience and enhance everyday comfort, such as family-friendly entertainment or casual dining.
Brand Loyal: Favours established, trustworthy brands that align with their practical and straightforward values.
Moderate Tech Adoption: Utilises technology selectively, focusing on tools that simplify their lives or enhance family engagement.
Happy Suburbanites.Bio: Dave runs an electrical services business in the southern suburbs of Perth.  He and his business partner started the business 20 years ago and now proudly lead a team of 10 staff.  Business has been good, providing both of them a fairly comfortable life.  Dave and his business partner share the managerial load which means he gets a few weekends off so he and his wife Jodie can get to a few Dockers games a year.  They have two grown up kids living at home with them who enjoy going to the footy with them – especially when Dad is picking up the tab. Dave has been a Fremantle fan even since they joined the AFL riding the highs and lows of the club.  He still remembers being in the outer cheering on the boys in their first final 2003 against the Bombers.  It hurt seeing them lose but he was confident than, and still is today, that there’s a flag not too far away – and he’ll be in the outer with Jodie and the kids cheering them on when they win the big one.
Happy Suburbanites.About Me:
Being a Fremantle Dockers member is important to me.
I like to be able to do things myself rather than pay someone else.
I always keeps things neat, tidy and ordered.
I have a strong belief in my own self-worth. 
I’m careful with how I  spend my money,

Happy Suburbanites.Cultural Diversity: Moderate: Happy Suburbanites have a slightly above average level of cultural diversity; a strong representation of people born in Australia and small but significant cohorts of UK and Asian backgrounds. Happy Suburbanites have a higher percentage of males to the Australian population but slightly less than the Fremantle member base.  Religious affiliations have a strong bias towards Christianity.
Happy Suburbanites.Sustainability: Moderate: Happy Suburbanites fans are aware of the importance of sustainability but are not always active in supporting sustainability initiatives. Where actions make good economic sense and fit comfortably within their lifestyle, they are more likely to get on board – investing in solar panels, recycling, conserving water, and safely disposing of household chemicals. They are also regular walkers and more likely than most to have made the switch to a hybrid or electric vehicle.
Happy Suburbanites.Political Values: Moderate to Conservative: Happy Suburbanites fans take a quiet, pragmatic approach to politics.  Generally conservative in outlook, they favour parties that reflect mainstream Australian values and a stable, no-nonsense approach to governing. They have little appetite for progressive causes and tend to judge parties on delivery rather than ideology – seeing few meaningful differences between the majors.
Happy Suburbanites.Outlook on Life: Negative to Neutral: Happy Suburbanites have a tendency towards a negative outlook, and are unlikely to see life getting much better for themselves or the world. They see themselves average in comparison to others, and are generally in good health. They focus more on consolidation than accumulation.

Modern Families.Profile: ”Modern Families" fans are most likely busy professionals juggling the demands of work, parenting, and homeownership. They are value-conscious and prioritise quality, often investing in products and services that simplify their hectic lives or enrich family time. With a forward-looking mindset, they embrace technology and sustainability while seeking a balance between practicality and enjoyment in their purchasing decisions.
Modern Families.Consumer Attributes:
”Modern Families" fans:
Family-Centered: Prioritises spending on products and services that enhance family life, such as education, childcare, and family activities.
Value-Conscious: Seeks quality and affordability, balancing budget constraints with the desire for durable and reliable products.
Tech-Savvy: Embraces technology to manage a busy lifestyle, favouring devices and platforms that offer convenience and connectivity.
Future-Oriented: Focused on investments that secures the family’s future, such as savings plans, health insurance, and homeownership.
Time-Poor but Driven: Balances professional careers with parenting, relying on efficient solutions like meal delivery, time-saving gadgets, or online services.
Health & Wellness Focused: Values health-conscious products for the family, including organic foods, fitness memberships, and wellness programs.
Environmentally Aware: Prefers brands/products that align with sustainable and eco-friendly values, reflecting concerns for their children’s future.
Socially Connected: Engages with online communities, parenting groups, and social networks for advice, inspiration, and shared experiences.
Modern Families.Bio: Michelle and Adam lead hectic lives both working in managerial roles whilst raising a young family.  Adam is a sales manager in the industrial sector and Michelle is a retail operations manager.  They share kid pickup duties from school and child-care and although they try to eat healthily, they sometimes find themselves stopping in for Maccas at the end of a busy day.  Adam is a native kiwi and passionate All Blacks, supporter, but when he moved to Australia it didn’t take long for him to fall in love with the Fremantle Dockers, and Michelle.  Heading to Optus Stadium to cheer on Freo with 6-year old Oliver is one of the few times a week they get real family time together.  When they’re not at the footy, they’re business renovating their first home or entertaining friends and family over a BBQ.  Any time Adam can, he hooks up his “tinny” to his SUV and heads out for some quality fishing time with his mates – as long as it doesn’t clash with a Freo home game.
Modern Families.About Me:
I‘m a proud and passionate Freo member.
I have a bigger social network than most people I know.
I’m always looking for new things to experience.
When I buy, I’m looking for the best quality at the lowest price.
Being able to enjoy the finer things in life is important to me.

Modern Families.Cultural Diversity: Moderate to High: Modern Families have a reasonable level of cultural diversity with a strong representation of Australia and / or New Zealand descent with multi-cultural groups from India and SE Asia.  Modern Families have a higher percentage of males to the Australian population and under index in females compared to the Fremantle member base.  Religiosity is not a significant influence.
Modern Families.Sustainability: Moderate: Modern Families fans are aware of the importance of Sustainability and worry about the future for their children. Practical in their approach, they buy organic, conserve water, drive hybrid or electric vehicles, and will pay a premium for carbon neutral products. They volunteer for community clean-ups and favour ethical investments, though formal environmental movement membership is less their style.
Modern Families.Political Values: Moderate: Modern Families fans have a moderate to low focus on political matters. They tend to be progressive in their attitudes and more likely to support parties aligned to their values and focused on green issues. When they do engage, it is typically driven by what matters closest to home – policies that protect the environment and secure a better future for their children.
Modern Families.Outlook on Life: Neutral to Positive: Modern Families have a reasonably  positive Outlook on Life and see themselves as somewhat better off than others.  They are generally in very good health.  Modern Families are quite optimistic about the future both for themselves and the world and feel that things are generally going in the right direction, if only everyone would do the right thing.  The cost of living is hurting now, but they can see a brighter future ahead.

Urban Hipster.Profile: ”Urban Hipster" fans are socially active, trend-conscious individuals who enjoy exploring new experiences and maintaining a stylish, modern lifestyle. They are career-focused and value independence, often spending on dining out, fitness, travel, and technology that enhances their image and convenience. With a strong preference for authenticity, they favour brands that align with their values of sustainability and creativity.
Urban Hipster.Consumer Attributes:
”Urban Hipster" fans:
Trend-Conscious: Keeps up with the latest fashion, technology, and cultural trends, often setting themselves apart with a distinctive style.
Experience-Seeking: Prefers spending on experiences like dining out, live music, and travel rather than material possessions.
Socially Active: Highly engaged in social activities, both in-person and online, valuing connections and building their personal brand.
Career-Driven: Focused on professional growth and financial independence, often investing in further education or career-enhancing tools.
Sustainability-Oriented: Favours brands and products that are environmentally friendly, ethically produced, and socially conscious.
Tech-Savvy: Quick to adopt new technology, especially if it enhances their lifestyle or aligns with their interests in creativity and convenience.
Urban Lifestyle: Enjoys the perks of inner-city living, such as proximity to cafes, cultural events, and boutique shopping.
Authenticity-Focused: Values originality and uniqueness in brands, favouring local, artisanal, or niche offerings over mainstream options.
Urban Hipster.Bio: Rebecca  was introduced to the Dockers by her dad Steve when she was 5 years old, and it quickly became part her DNA.  She loves the club and loves the social side of being part of the Purple army and attending most games with her best mates.  But sometimes work and life can get so busy that there just isn’t enough time to go to the footy.  Rebecca worked in Melbourne for a few years after graduating, before heading back to her beloved Fremantle, away from Melbourne winters and back with family and friends.  Working as a People & Culture manager in an ad agency keeps Rebecca very busy, but not so busy that she doesn’t have time for fun with friends and exploring new life experiences.  Rebecca is a career woman who enjoys being financially independent and in control over her future.  Rebecca rents an apartment in Northbridge close to work, her gym, pubs, bars, restaurants, and most importantly the beach.  Life is good and the only thing that could make it better would be a Freo premiership!

Urban Hipster.Cultural Diversity: Moderate to High: Urban Hipsters members have a moderate to high level of cultural diversity with good representation of people born outside Australia, second generation Australians, and / or have worked overseas.  Urban Hipsters have a higher percentage of males to the Australian population and slightly more than the Fremantle member base.  Religious affiliations are also diverse which is consistent with the cultural background of Urban Hipsters members.
Urban Hipster.Sustainability: High: Urban Hipsters members have a high level of awareness and interest in Sustainability and invest time to ensure they are well informed. Socially aware and environmentally conscious, they are most likely to volunteer, join environmental movements, and back politicians on their green credentials – extending their values into ethical investing, structured fitness, and mindful practices like yoga and meditation.
Urban Hipster.Political Values: Moderate to Progressive: Urban Hipsters members take a genuine but measured interest in politics – progressive in outlook and drawn to parties with strong environmental and social credentials, without being vocal or dogmatic about their views. When it comes to casting a vote, backing politicians who champion green policy and deliver meaningful social good is what matters most to this segment.
Urban Hipster.Outlook on Life: Positive: Urban Hipsters members have a pretty positive Outlook on Life and see themselves as generally pretty lucky to enjoy the life they lead, which they think is better off than others.  They are generally very good in health and lead an active and healthy lifestyle. Urban Hipsters members are optimistic about the future both for themselves and the world and look forward to what’s ahead.
